  "@context": "https://schema.org",

  "title": "Account / Operations Executive",
  "description": "Doheney Services Limited helps businesses work more efficiently and effectively by providing tech, admin, and structured staff, all within the budget provided. The company has been working with businesses in Nigeria, the UK, and some other parts of the world, to help them do better work for less money. Doheney services can take over tedious tasks like hiring new suitable employees, project management, or even creating reward systems.

We are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position: Account / Operations Executive

Job Location:&nbsp;Ilupeju, Lagos
Employment Type: Contract&nbsp;(Hybrid)
Duration: 6 Months (Maternity Cover)

Role Overview


	We are seeking a detail-oriented Accountant with administrative and operational experience to join our team for a 6-month maternity cover.
	The ideal candidate will bridge the gap between office management and financial reporting, ensuring that daily operations run smoothly while maintaining accurate financial records.


Key Responsibilities
Financial Support &amp; Accounting:


	Manage petty cash disbursements and maintain accurate digital logs.
	Prepare and process invoices, receipts, and basic financial statements.
	Assist with monthly payroll processing and statutory remittance (PAYE, Pension, etc.).
	Perform bank reconciliations and track accounts payable/receivable.


Office Administration:


	Oversee daily office operations, including facility maintenance and vendor management.
	Procure office supplies and manage inventory to prevent stockouts.
	Manage executive calendars, schedule meetings, and coordinate travel logistics if required.
	Maintain a robust physical and digital filing system.


Operational Coordination:


	Liaise with external vendors to ensure service standards are met.
	Support the implementation of internal operational policies.
	Serve as the primary point of contact for internal inquiries and visitor management.


Requirements &amp; Qualifications


	Education: BSc / HND&nbsp;qualification in Accounting, Business Administration, or a related field.
	Experience: 2 - 4 years of experience in an Administrative or Operations role with direct exposure to bookkeeping / Accounting.
	Tech Savvy: Proficiency in Microsoft Excel and accounting software (e.g., QuickBooks, Xero, or Sage) is highly preferred.


Salary
N200,000 - N250,000 Net Monthly.

Method of Application&nbsp;
Interested and qualified candidates should send their CV to:&nbsp;eosanyintuyi@doheneyservices.com&nbsp;using the Job Position as the subject of the mail.",

The company has been working with businesses in Nigeria, the UK, and some other parts of the world, to help them do better work for less money. Doheney services can take over tedious tasks like hiring new suitable employees, project management, or even creating reward systems.

We are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position: Account / Operations Executive

Job Location: Ilupeju, Lagos
Employment Type: Contract (Hybrid)
Duration: 6 Months (Maternity Cover)

Role Overview

	The ideal candidate will bridge the gap between office management and financial reporting, ensuring that daily operations run smoothly while maintaining accurate financial records.


Key Responsibilities
Financial Support & Accounting:


	Manage petty cash disbursements and maintain accurate digital logs.
	Prepare and process invoices, receipts, and basic financial statements.
	Assist with monthly payroll processing and statutory remittance (PAYE, Pension, etc.).
	Perform bank reconciliations and track accounts payable/receivable.

	Oversee daily office operations, including facility maintenance and vendor management.
	Procure office supplies and manage inventory to prevent stockouts.
	Manage executive calendars, schedule meetings, and coordinate travel logistics if required.
	Maintain a robust physical and digital filing system.


Operational Coordination:


	Liaise with external vendors to ensure service standards are met.
	Support the implementation of internal operational policies.
	Serve as the primary point of contact for internal inquiries and visitor management.

	Education: BSc / HND qualification in Accounting, Business Administration, or a related field.

  "@context": "https://schema.org",

  "title": "Open Market Sales Representative",
  "description": "J3 is an FMCG distributor. We have been in existence over 8 years.&nbsp;J3 has branches in several locations; Lagos, Ibadan, Ogun, Ondo, and Osun state.

We are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position:&nbsp;&nbsp;Open Market Sales Representative

Job Location:&nbsp;Iyana, Ipaja - Lagos
Employment Type: Full-time

Job Summary


	The Open Market Sales Representative is responsible for driving product penetration, sales growth, and brand dominance in open markets.
	The role involves prospecting new customers, taking orders, managing trade relationships, ensuring proper merchandising, and executing trade marketing activities across designated markets.
	This position focuses on increasing product visibility, expanding market share, and ensuring consistent product availability in targeted open-market clusters.


Key Responsibilities
Sales Execution &amp; Market Penetration:


	Achieve daily, weekly, and monthly sales targets.
	Prospect, open, and register new outlets in assigned clusters (markets, kiosks, roadside stalls, mini-marts, etc.).
	Actively sell products to wholesalers, retailers, market vendors, and sub-distributors.


Route-to-Market &amp; Territory Growth:


	Execute planned market route coverage and ensure daily presence in assigned territory.
	Conduct market mapping and establish a strong customer database.


Customer Relationship Management:


	Build and maintain long-term trade relationships to increase loyalty and repeat purchases.
	Educate customers on product benefits, promotions, pricing, and policies.


Merchandising &amp; Brand Visibility:


	Ensure proper product display, pricing, and stocking level in the open market.


Reporting &amp; Compliance:


	Submit accurate daily sales reports, order records, and customer account updates.
	Ensure compliance with all company sales, cash handling, and operational procedures.


Qualifications


	Interested candidates should possess an OND qualification with 3-5 years of experience.


Salary
N120,000 / Month.

Method of Application
Interested and qualified candidates should send their CV to:&nbsp;midaasconsult@gmail.com&nbsp;using the Job Position and Location as the subject of the email.

Open Market Sales Representative Job at J3 Foods and Store International




        
    

        
        J3 is an FMCG distributor. We have been in existence over 8 years. J3 has branches in several locations; Lagos, Ibadan, Ogun, Ondo, and Osun state.

We are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position:  Open Market Sales Representative

Job Location: Iyana, Ipaja - Lagos
Employment Type: Full-time

Job Summary
     

        



     (adsbygoogle = window.adsbygoogle || []).push({});
    
    




	The Open Market Sales Representative is responsible for driving product penetration, sales growth, and brand dominance in open markets.
	The role involves prospecting new customers, taking orders, managing trade relationships, ensuring proper merchandising, and executing trade marketing activities across designated markets.
	This position focuses on increasing product visibility, expanding market share, and ensuring consistent product availability in targeted open-market clusters.


Key Responsibilities
Sales Execution & Market Penetration:


	Achieve daily, weekly, and monthly sales targets.
	Prospect, open, and register new outlets in assigned clusters (markets, kiosks, roadside stalls, mini-marts, etc.).
	Actively sell products to wholesalers, retailers, market vendors, and sub-distributors.


Route-to-Market & Territory Growth:


	Execute planned market route coverage and ensure daily presence in assigned territory.
	Conduct market mapping and establish a strong customer database.


Customer Relationship Management:


	Build and maintain long-term trade relationships to increase loyalty and repeat purchases.
	Educate customers on product benefits, promotions, pricing, and policies.


Merchandising & Brand Visibility:


	Ensure proper product display, pricing, and stocking level in the open market.


Reporting & Compliance:


	Submit accurate daily sales reports, order records, and customer account updates.
	Ensure compliance with all company sales, cash handling, and operational procedures.


Qualifications


	Interested candidates should possess an OND qualification with 3-5 years of experience.


Salary
N120,000 / Month.

Method of Application
Interested and qualified candidates should send their CV to: [email protected] using the Job Position and Location as the subject of the email.

Note: For more information, contact on WhatsApp via: 09152781100.

We are Dynamic, Responsive, Ethically Responsible, Proactive and Growing. We Believe in Creating Values and Making a Difference

We are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position: Accountant

Job Location:&nbsp;Jabi, Abuja (FCT)&nbsp;
Employment Type: Full-time

Job Summary


	J.A.K. Energy Group is seeking for a highly skilled and experienced Senior Accountant to join our team.
	The ideal candidate will be responsible for managing financial records, ensuring compliance with regulatory standards, and supporting strategic financial decisions. Strong expertise in trade finance and professional accounting certification are essential for this role.


Key Responsibilities
Financial Record Keeping &amp; Reporting:


	Maintain accurate financial records of all transactions
	Prepare monthly, quarterly, and annual financial statements
	Monitor and report on financial performance.


Accounts Payable &amp; Receivable:


	Manage invoicing, payments, and collections
	Ensure timely settlement of obligations and follow-up on receivables.


Bank Reconciliation &amp; Cash Management:


	Reconcile bank statements and resolve discrepancies
	Monitor cash flow and ensure adequate liquidity.


Trade Finance Operations:


	Handle trade finance instruments such as Letters of Credit (LC), bank guarantees, and bills for collection
	Process import and export transactions in line with agreed trade terms.


Trade Documentation &amp; Compliance:


	Review and verify trade documents (invoices, bill of lading, packing list, etc.)
	Ensure compliance with international trade regulations and internal policies.


Liaison with Banks &amp; Financial Institutions:


	Coordinate with banks on trade finance facilities and transactions
	Follow up on approvals, payments, and documentation.


Foreign Transactions &amp; Exchange Monitoring:


	Manage foreign currency transactions
	Monitor exchange rate impacts and ensure proper recording.


Budgeting, Forecasting &amp; Financial Analysis:


	Support budgeting and financial planning processes
	Provide financial insights and analysis for decision-making.


Risk Management &amp; Internal Controls:


	Identify financial and trade-related risks
	Implement controls to minimize exposure and ensure compliance.


Audit &amp; Regulatory Compliance:


	Prepare documentation for audits
	Ensure adherence to accounting standards and regulatory requirements.


Cross-Functional Collaboration:


	Work with procurement, logistics, and operations teams
	Align financial records with supply chain and trade activities.


Requirements


	Professional certification (ICAN is mandatory)
	Minimum of 5 years&rsquo; relevant accounting experience
	Strong knowledge of trade finance operations
	Proficiency in accounting software and Microsoft Excel
	Excellent analytical and problem-solving skills
	High level of integrity and attention to detail
	Strong communication and interpersonal skills.


Method of Application&nbsp;

Job Description


	JAK Energy Group is seeking a detail- oriented and experienced Internal Auditor to evaluate and improve the effectiveness of risk management , control, and governance processes withinin the organization.
	The ideal candidate will ensure compliance with internal policies, regulatory requirements, and industry standards.
	The ideal candiddate will audit all the departments of the organization.


Key Responsibilities
Risk Assessment (Operational &amp; Supply Chain Focus):


	Proactively identify, evaluate, and prioritize risk areas across critical business units particularly LPG procurement, depot operations, logistics, asset management, and pricing activities.
	This includes detecting risks related to price manipulation, stock discrepancies, supply delays, vendor reliability, and safety compliance failures, ensuring early mitigation before operational impact.


Internal Control Evaluation (Process &amp; System Integrity):


	Assess and validate the effectiveness of internal controls embedded within daily reporting systems, Google Form inputs, dashboards, and operational workflows.
	Ensure that controls around data entry accuracy, approval hierarchies, transaction validation, and system automation logic are functioning correctly and cannot be bypassed or manipulated.


Compliance Verification (Industry &amp; Regulatory Alignment):


	Ensure strict adherence to oil &amp; gas industry regulations, DPR/NMDPRA standards, safety protocols, and internal SOPs across all operational touchpoints.
	This includes compliance in offloading procedures, plant safety inspections, pricing disclosures, and vendor engagements, minimizing legal and reputational exposure.


Operational Audits (Field &amp; Performance-Based Reviews):


	Conduct detailed audits of plant operations, LPG supply chain activities, staff reporting performance, and depot efficiency.
	Evaluate operational workflows suchas pre-offloading checks, offloading execution, daily operations logs, and asset condition reports to identify inefficiencies, bottlenecks, or performance gaps.


Performance Intelligence &amp; Staff Productivity Analysis:


	Leverage the company&rsquo;s dashboard and reporting systems to evaluate staff output using weighted activity scoring models (e.g., higher weight for critical tasks like system creation, procurement execution, or incident resolution).
	Identify top performers, average contributors, and underperforming staff, enabling data-driven performance management


Incident &amp; CAPA Oversight (Centralized Issue Management):


	Ensure that all operational issues from PM, CA, Daily Ops, Inspections, Pre- Offloading, Offloading, and Asset Condition reports are properly captured within the Incident &amp; CAPA system.
	Track root causes, corrective actions, and closure timelines to ensure a closed-loop resolution system.


Reporting &amp; Continuous Improvement Tracking:


	Prepare structured audit and performance reports highlighting key risks, operational gaps, compliance issues, and improvement opportunities.
	Present actionable recommendations to management and conduct follow-up audits to verify implementation and impact.


Data Analysis &amp; Business Intelligence Integration:


	Analyze operational and financial datasets including LPG pricing trends, vendor data, supply distribution, and staff reports to identify patterns, anomalies, and optimization opportunities.
	Support management decision-making with real-time insights from dashboards and predictive indicators


Requirements


	Masters in Accounting, Finance, or related field
	Professional certification (e.g., ACA, ACCA, CIA) is an added advantage
	Minimum of 5 years experience in auditing or related role
	Strong analytical and problem-solving skills
	Excellent attention to detail
	Good communication and report-writing skills


Skills &amp; Competencies:


	High level of integrity and professionalism
	Knowledge of audit standards and procedures
	Strong organizational and time management skills
	Ability to work independently and within a team.
